# Global Health Access Trust

> A discreet, high-trust private capital platform that structures verified health funding and delivery for underserved populations in crisis and conflict zones.

Global Health Access Trust connects serious philanthropic capital with verified health interventions. The platform operates a closed engagement model: donors, volunteers, and administrators work through role-based portals, with all contact mediated by staff. There are no public donations or open sign-ups.

## Pages

- [/](/): Institutional homepage introducing the trust, its mission, and current focus areas.
- [About](/about): Overview of the trust's purpose, values, and operational approach.
- [About the Trust](/about-the-trust): Detailed background on the organisation's foundation and mandate.
- [What We Do](/what-we-do): Summary of programmes, funding mechanisms, and intervention types.
- [Our Work](/our-work): Field-level examples and delivery partners.
- [How We Work](/how-we-work): Governance, verification, and operational processes.
- [Learn About Our Mission](/learn-about-our-mission): Mission statement and strategic priorities.
- [Commissioned Projects](/commissioned-projects): The five-step bespoke project process for major donors.
- [Get Involved](/get-involved): Information on professional and institutional engagement pathways.
- [Volunteers](/volunteers): Overview of the volunteer professional network and application pipeline.
- [Support the Trust](/support-the-trust): Guidance for qualified donors and institutional partners.
- [Donate](/donate): Structured funding engagement information for eligible contributors.
- [Trustee Biographies](/trustee-biographies): Profiles of the board and advisory trustees.
- [Governance](/governance): Board structure, oversight, and accountability framework.
- [Governance and Legal Framework](/governance-legal-framework): Constitutional and legal basis of the trust.
- [Constitution](/constitution): The trust's governing constitutional document.
- [Financial Controls](/financial-controls): Policies for financial stewardship and audit.
- [Risk Management](/risk-management): Approach to operational, financial, and reputational risk.
- [Safeguarding](/safeguarding): Safeguarding policy for beneficiaries, staff, and partners.
- [Anti-Fraud](/anti-fraud): Fraud prevention, detection, and reporting procedures.
- [Conflict of Interest](/conflict-of-interest): Policy for managing conflicts among trustees and staff.
- [Whistleblowing](/whistleblowing): Confidential reporting channels and protections.
- [Donor Recognition](/donor-recognition): How contributions are acknowledged and reported.
- [Donor Guide](/donor-guide): Practical information for engaged donors.
- [Publications](/publications): Reports, impact summaries, and public documents.
- [Blog](/blog): Articles and updates from the trust.
- [Blog Post](/blog/ukraine-health-access-update): Representative update on Ukraine health access work.
- [Blog Post](/blog/multicultural-field-teams): Representative field report on multicultural delivery teams.
- [Contact](/contact): Secure enquiry form for professional and institutional contact.
- [Contact the Trust](/contact-the-trust): Alternative contact route for trustees and advisors.
- [Frequently Asked Questions](/frequently-asked-questions): Common questions about engagement and operations.
- [Privacy Policy](/privacy-policy): GDPR-compliant privacy notice and data handling.
- [Terms of Use](/terms-of-use): Terms governing use of the website and services.
- [Cookie Policy](/cookie-policy): Cookie and tracking technology notice.
- [Accessibility Statement](/accessibility-statement): Accessibility standards and support.
- [Data Access Request](/data-access-request): Form for exercising GDPR data subject rights.
